name: Chicken Madras-India
recipeIngredient: 6 (more for a vindaloo) whole small Dried chiles (bird's Eye chiles)
2/3 of a batch of basic curry sauce
1 bunch Methi (dried fenugreek leaves), washed in a sieve
-then left moist for at least 10 minutes
recipeInstructions:
This is a hot, simple curry. Increase the number of dried chiles for a vindaloo.
1. Heat a little oil in a large heavy frying pan. Fry chicken pieces over moderate heat until they are sealed and have turned white. Remove them from the pan and set aside.
2. Heat the rest of the oil in a heavy pan over moderate heat.
3. Add whole dried chiles and fry until they start to swell.
4. Turn the heat to low. Let the pan cool slightly, then add chile powder, cumin, and coriander. Stir for 10 seconds continuously (do not burn; it will taste bitter).
5. Quickly put in the curry sauce, salt, and chicken pieces. Cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Ten minutes before the end of cooking, add garam masala and fenugreek leaves.
7. Simmer gently for the last 10 minutes, stirring frequently. The sauce should thicken.
